observations.recommendation: Further, we ardently recommended that Management prioritize / commit to undertake the cleansing of PPE account balance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.reason: The management is yet to plan how to conduct the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

Create a team to conduct the immediate inventory of all lots purchased and expedite the processing of Certificates of Land Title and other legal documents to support LGU’s ownership;
observations.reason: The management is still conducting land inventory and processing land titling. | No team created yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to: 

a. Require the Municipal Development Council and the budget committee to judiciously plan and identify the Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund and include only those that are feasible and could be accomplished within the target date of completion;

b. Fully implement the programmed development PPAs during the year as embodied in the Annual Investment/ Development Plan and ensure optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the benefit of the constituents of the Municipality;
c. Strictly observe proper timelines and install effective monitoring of fund utilization and implementation;

d. Ensure that all savings from completed projects and all projects that are no longer feasible and not yet implemented are considered for immediate realignment subject to budgetary rules or reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the 20% Development Fund for re-appropriation to other development projects.

We recommended that Management:

a. Stop utilizing the Special Trust Fund for the unspent LDRRMF of the previous years without a duly approved AIP by the local Sanggunian and the LCE;
b. Strictly adhere to Section 6.5 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01 and Section of 5.1.2 COA Circular No. 2021-002;

c. Require the LDRRMC to identify projects, programs, and activities to be funded from the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund and incorporate these PPAs as a separate caption of the LGU’s LDRRMFIP subject to the approval by the Sanggunian and the Municipal Mayor pursuant to Section 6.5 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01; and

d. Regularly monitor the balances of the STF and revert back to the General Fund any unutilized amount after five (5) years which will be used fund for social services NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Human Resources Office, exert more efforts to fill the LDRRM Officer II position and augment staff in the LDRRM Office in order to achieve full implementation of DRRM programs, projects, and activities for the maximum benefit of constituents and to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.
observations.reason: The position remains vacant because the CSC disapproved the appointment of the previous LDRRMO due to an error in the job posting. Additionally, the election ban has prevented management from creating a new job posting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management review and correct the existing procurement process and ensure that meticulous and judicious procurement planning is undertaken, including the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and that procurement of goods should be done on competitive bidding pursuant to Sections 7 and 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. The Municipality may resort to alternative methods of procurement whenever justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of the same IRR.
observations.reason: The management will review its Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Strictly observe that all procurement of POL products shall be done through competitive bidding, except when the LGU has determined that Negotiated Procurement - Direct Retail Purchase is the best modality for the procurement of non-bulk POL products. The BAC, through a resolution, shall justify and recommend the use of such method.

If Direct Retail Purchase is used, the BAC and the Municipal Mayor shall then issue a Resolution delegating only to specific officials, personnel, committee or office in the LGU the conduct of such method pursuant to guidelines set forth in Annex H of the Updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
observations.reason: The management opted for Direct Contracting as their procurement method deeming it the most feasible option.

observations.recommendation: Require Municipal Accountant to conduct orientation with the designated officials, personnel, committee or office regarding withholding taxes and the corresponding issuance of the Certificate of Withheld Taxes (CWT).

We recommended that Management evaluate the LGU’s current organizational structure and manning complement and consider adding personnel to the Accounting Unit and observe faithfully the reglementary periods set on the submission of accounts and reports for a prompt audit and early reporting of audit results.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Make the required adjusting entries to properly recognize the pertinent PPE account;
Record the appropriate depreciation expense based on the capitalized value of these PPE; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to clarify any uncertainties as to whether a project qualifies as a minor or major repair, or if it is a completely new or different project.

We recommended and Management concurred to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Accounts Payable balance by recording unreported obligations and their corresponding expense accounts, and reverse any obligations incorrectly charged, ensuring the financial statements accurately reflect the municipality's financial position and comply with applicable accounting standards;
Prioritize the payment of these obligations by deferring non-priority expenditures and implementing austerity measures to break the cycle of charging prior year's expenses to the current appropriation; and
Coordinate with the national agencies whose electricity accounts are currently paid by the LGU and request that these bills be charged to their own budgets, either temporarily or permanently, to help reduce costs.

We recommended and Management is amenable of the following:
Instruct the BAC to ensure the timely submission of all contracts within the prescribed timeline and establish a monitoring system to track compliance with submission requirements;
Designate a focal person in the Office of the BAC, GSO and the MEO to take charge in the systematic compilation of complete contract and supporting documents for government projects; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit inspection requests, along with the required supporting documents, to COA for all 100% completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended and it was agreed by Management to:
Instruct the Accounting Office to thoroughly verify all supporting documents before certifying their completeness to prevent delays in submitting these documents to the COA Office;
Stop processing payments with incomplete supporting documents; and
Strictly observe the reglementary periods set for the submission of accounts and reports for a prompt audit and early reporting of audit results.
We recommended that Management finish its annual physical count of its property, plant and equipment and submit a copy of the same to COA on or before January 31 of each year;
We also recommended that Management reconcile its PPE Subsidiary Ledgers and its property cards regularly, if needed; and

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

i. Coordinate with the Department of Education – Division of Misamis Oriental regarding land assets whose tax declaration certificates were already in the name of the schools. Confirm whether these assets were already recorded in the agency’s books and request documentation to support the dropping of assets from the LGU’s books. Otherwise, prepare and forward to the agency the necessary documents to transfer the assets, such as the Deed of Donation, and drop the said assets from the LGU’s books. Either way, submit the corresponding journal entry voucher (JEV) to COA; and

ii. Prepare inventory list/report of all land properties of the LGU based on available records. As soon as the actual inventory is completed, reconcile differences and disclose in the Notes to FS the relevant information as required in IPSAS 17.
observations.reason: The LGU recorded JEV#100-2412-2242 to recognize land school site with original title/deed of donation (DR: Government Equity – 6,350.00; CR: Land – 6,350.00)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Prepare and submit the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) with the supporting documents to recognize in the books of accounts the following:

i. Borrowing costs incurred totaling ₱2,174,555.04 as part of the cost of the building;

ii. Current and prior year depreciation expenses for the municipal hall building; and
observations.managementAction:
The municipal accountant has made the necessary adjustment in JEV#100-2402-0371 (DR: Buildings - 2,174,555.04 
CR: Prior Period Adjustment - 1,554,806.88; Accumulate Depreciation-Buildings - 619,748.16)

observations.recommendation: b. Adhere strictly to the recognition and disclosure requirements mandated by IPSAS No. 5 for any future incurring of borrowing costs.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant already made the necessary adjustment through the recording of JEV.
Reiterated in this report.
Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to review and correct the existing procurement process and ensure that procurement of goods should be done through competitive bidding, except as provided in Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. Also, ensure to procure only from duly registered suppliers; and

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments to suppliers/contractors should be supported with complete documentation, including contracts/purchase orders, and valid official receipts as proof of payment.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant ensures that payments were supported by complete documentation before submitting it to the auditor’s office.

observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the deadline for the submission of the AAPSI, which is within 60 days after the receipt of the AAR, without the need for verbal or written notices from this office. Also, assess or align the capabilities of manpower in each unit or department, and designate a focal person to ensure the timely discharge of functions, especially in the compliance and reporting requirements covered in the AAPSI and the monitoring thereof.

We recommended that the Accountant and Treasurer to:

Conduct immediate reconciliation of records and make the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable including its corresponding Deferred Income accounts and submit to the audit team the approved JEV together with its supporting documents including the working papers. Treasurer should provide a complete list of taxpayers and the corresponding amount of uncollected delinquent taxes necessary for the establishment of the subsidiary ledgers;
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer reconciled their balances and necessary adjustments were made through the recording of JEV No. 100-2405-0949 and 100-2405-0950.

observations.recommendation: Accounting Office should adhere to the recording requirements of the NGAS for the Real Property and Special Education Tax, especially on the basis of setting up the total RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year. This also requires the Treasurer to provide accurate and reliable amount of RPT/SET collectibles at the beginning of each year; and
observations.reason: The Accounting Office obtains the Schedule Tax Collectible for the Current Year and the Certified List of All RPT Delinquencies from the Treasury Office.

observations.recommendation: Undertake periodic reconciliation of records to ensure correct and reliable balances.
We recommended that Management evaluate its existing policy for the determination of wage rate of Job Order personnel and establish guidelines to adhere to the minimum standards on labor practices.
observations.reason: The management implemented an increase in the salary of Job Order Personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Create a team to conduct the immediate inventory of all lots purchased and expedite the processing of Certificates of Land Title and other legal documents to support LGU’s ownership;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Coordinate with the Department of Education – Division of Misamis Oriental regarding land assets whose tax declaration certificates were already in the name of the schools. Confirm whether these assets were already recorded in the agency’s books and request documentation to support the dropping of assets from the LGU’s books. Otherwise, prepare and forward to the agency the necessary documents to transfer the assets, such as the Deed of Donation, and drop the said assets from the LGU’s books. Either way, submit the corresponding journal entry voucher (JEV) to COA; and
Prepare inventory list/report of all land properties of the LGU based on available records. As soon as the actual inventory is completed, reconcile differences and disclose in the Notes to FS the relevant information as required in IPSAS 17.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare and submit the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) with the supporting documents to recognize in the books of accounts the following:
Borrowing costs incurred totaling P2,174,555 as part of the cost of the building;
Current and prior year depreciation expenses for the municipal hall building; and
Adhere strictly to the recognition and disclosure requirements mandated by IPSAS No. 5 for any future incurring of borrowing costs.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Human Resources Office, exert more efforts to fill the LDRRM Officer II position and augment staff in the LDRRM Office in order to achieve full implementation of DRRM programs, projects, and activities for the maximum benefit of constituents and to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.

We recommended that Management review and correct the existing procurement process and ensure that meticulous and judicious procurement planning is undertaken, including the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and that procurement of goods should be done on competitive bidding pursuant to Sections 7 and 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. The Municipality may resort to alternative methods of procurement whenever justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of the same IRR.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly observe that all procurement of POL products shall be done through competitive bidding, except when the LGU has determined that Negotiated Procurement - Direct Retail Purchase is the best modality for the procurement of non-bulk POL products. The BAC, through a resolution, shall justify and recommend the use of such method.
If Direct Retail Purchase is used, the BAC and the Municipal Mayor shall then issue a Resolution delegating only to specific officials, personnel, committee or office in the LGU the conduct of such method pursuant to guidelines set forth in Annex H of the Updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
Require Municipal Accountant to conduct orientation with the designated officials, personnel, committee or office regarding withholding taxes and the corresponding issuance of the Certificate of Withheld Taxes (CWT).

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to review and correct the existing procurement process and ensure that procurement of goods should be done through competitive bidding, except as provided in Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. Also, ensure to procure only from duly registered suppliers; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments to suppliers/contractors should be supported with complete documentation, including contracts/purchase orders, and valid official receipts as proof of payment.

We recommended that Management evaluate the LGU’s current organizational structure and manning complement and consider adding personnel to the Accounting Unit and observe faithfully the reglementary periods set on the submission of accounts and reports for a prompt audit and early reporting of audit results.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the deadline for the submission of the AAPSI, which is within 60 days after the receipt of the AAR, without the need for verbal or written notices from this office. Also, assess or align the capabilities of manpower in each unit or department, and designate a focal person to ensure the timely discharge of functions, especially in the compliance and reporting requirements covered in the AAPSI and the monitoring thereof.

CY 2022 Finding No. 3 | We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Account to eliminate reciprocal accounts for purposes of consolidation of the funds maintained by the LGU in order to reflect the actual receivable and payable of the Municipality. Furnish COA with a copy of the revised Consolidated Financial statements, together with the eliminating entries made.
observations.reason: Interfund balances were fully eliminated as presented in the LGU’s financial statements.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2020 Finding No. 2 | We recommended that the Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory taking and prepare/submit the RPCI to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for verification of the reported balances in the financial statements and the Accountant for reconciliation of balances with the SLCs in accordance with Section 13, Chapter 8 of GAM Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Management opted to purchase office supplies and other inventoriable items only when needed. Inventories are recorded as expense as soon as inventories are issued to end-users.
observations.reason: Management opted to purchase office supplies and other inventoriable items only when needed. Inventories are recorded as expense as soon as inventories are issued to end-users.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2020 Finding No. 11 | We recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions in Section 271 of RA 7160 and release the RPT shares of the 15 Barangays immediately on a quarterly basis.
observations.managementAction: RPT shares of the 15 Barangays were made on a quarterly basis.
observations.reason: RPT shares of the 15 Barangays were made on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management adopt a uniform property identification system for PPE wherein a unique Property Number shall be assigned for each PPE item pursuant to the above-mentioned circular. For easy identification, the Property Number shall be prominently shown in the property sticker, in addition to the following vital information on the PPE item:

a. Description of the property
b. Model Number
c. Serial Number
d. Acquisition Date/Cost
e. Person Accountable
f. Space for the validation/signature of the Inventory Committee

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Department make necessary adjusting entries to record the accrued liabilities pertaining to earned leave benefits of the employees for the current and previous years after ensuring the correctness of the accumulated leave credit balances as of December 31, 2021.  Review at each reporting date and adjust to reflect the current best estimate to ensure that employee’s benefits are properly accounted for and disclosed in the financial statements.

We recommended that the management exercise prudence in the conduct of procurement process. Further, instruct the BAC to verify at once the exact location of the business address of the concerned entity, and notify the same to present proof of technical eligibilities, otherwise consider disqualification.

We recommended that the management review its existing control procedures particular to the procurement of petroleum, oil and lubricant. Further, instruct the accounting to avoid reimbursement to personnel or prepayment to supplier of the said claims being not in accordance with existing rules and procedure which might be subjected to future disallowances.

We recommended that the management secure contract with the service providers to protect the interest of the government considering that the purposes of travel can be anticipated. Further, ensure that necessary documents such as proof of receipt and those listed above are submitted to support the claim.

We recommended that the Management observe the proper utilization of the 20% DF pursuant to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1 consistent with the government’s objectives.
We recommended that the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay under the General Provisions of GAA should be strictly observed.

We recommended that Management facilitate the creation of a disposal committee to oversee the disposal activities of the agency’s unserviceable properties which include determination of the disposable properties, determination of appraisal values and mode of disposal.  Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for unserviceable properties and instruct the accounting department to facilitate the dropping of the unserviceable items in the agency records. Further, demand return of missing PPE items from the concerned accountable personnel and apply administrative sanctions to personnel found to have malice over appropriating government property for personal use.

We recommended that the Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory taking and prepare/submit the RPCI to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for verification of the reported balances in the financial statements and the Accountant for reconciliation of balances with the SLCs in accordance with Section 13, Chapter 8 of GAM Volume I.

We recommended that the management require the accountant to review the long outstanding/stale checks, and require the designated cashier to cancel those unclaimed checks that became stale if there be any. Prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to record the cancellation of these checks. Further, notify the payees to return the stale checks in their possession for replacement if possible, otherwise consider the reversion of these payables to the unappropriated surplus of the fund against which it was previously charged as mandated in the said P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the balances of the reciprocal accounts under the three Funds and book the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement. Also, prepare and file a request for write off for those long dormant accounts as prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission.

We recommended that the management submit the aforementioned supporting documents relative to the said repairs and maintenance disbursements. Further, request from the auditor’s office to conduct inspection of the damaged/repaired units as indicated in the Waste Material Report.

We recommended that management review the dormant bank accounts and determine whether the purpose of these accounts had been fully accomplished. Further, consider closing inactive bank accounts and consolidate into one single account if eyed for future programs such as equity requirements, otherwise remit the amount to the National Treasury as required by the aforementioned circular.

We recommended that the management strictly monitor the utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Ensure that previous cash advances are liquidated prior to granting anew pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommend that the management ensure that all payments should be made by check directly payable to the suppliers, hence, stop granting reimbursement of purchases to Municipal personnel. Establish a Petty Cash Fund for the purpose of accommodating those petty expenses.

We recommend that the Management comply with the pertinent provisions of the said statutory law. Further, request the issuing entity to provide valid official receipts and necessary justifications of the undertakings, otherwise the same shall be recalled for forfeiture in favor of the government notwithstanding possible legal recourse.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation on why they entered into a contract for the procurement of the said parcel of land despite the insufficiency of appropriation.

We recommended that the lacking supporting documents pertaining to the procurement of the said land be submitted. Also, submit a written certification/resolution from the provincial appraisal committee that the fair market value used was proper and correct.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the whole amount of the land.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the above-mentioned erroneous recording and to reflect the correct amount of the CIP account.
We recommended that completed projects recorded under Construction in Progress should be reclassified and booked up to its appropriate asset.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the unrecorded retention money amounting to P76,715.80.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the said Accounts Payable to the Trust Liabilities account.

We recommended that management require the Property Supply Officer to maintain stock cards to record purchases and issuances of inventories, prepare and submit RSMI to the Accounting Office to support the dropping from the books of all issued and consumed drugs and medicines, supplies and materials. The RSMI should be prepared weekly and shall be used by the Accounting Unit as a basis in preparing the JEV to record the supplies and materials issued.
We recommended that Management observe the proper utilization of the 20% DF pursuant to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to attain the government’s objectives.

We recommended that Management observe the proper utilization of the 5% LDRRMF pursuant to the pertinent provisions of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG Joint Memorandum Circulars No. 2013-1 and 2014-1 and ensure that only disaster risk reduction and management related activities are charged against the said fund.

We recommended that Management process the disbursing voucher/claim on the advance payment/mobilization fee only upon submission of any form of security by the contractors as prescribed in Section 4.2 of the Annex E of IRR-RA 9184. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documents that are required in COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions in Section 271 of RA 7160 and release the RPT shares of the 15 Barangays immediately on a quarterly basis.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions specified under COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 362 of the GAAM Manual Volume I, and that all prospective claims relative to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles be properly supported with pertinent documents to substantiate their validity, propriety and legality.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government vehicles subjected to repairs.

Require that the Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste Material be prepared by the Supply Officer and approved by the LCE.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a ledger card for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is economically repairable.
observations.reason: Payments for repairs and maintenance of government vehicle were still not properly documented. It was noted that repairs done on various days were processed and paid in one disbursement voucher with only one PR, PO and other supporting documents and without specifying the vehicle being repaired. Pre-inspection report were attached but these were not informative and did not indicate the materials needed to be repaired/replaced and why. Ledger cards per vehicle were also not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the persons responsible to conduct an inventory of its local roads and enjoin the other department heads for the compliance of this circular.
observations.reason: Recognized Road Networks pertained only to recently completed projects but those that were already completed in previous years were still not recognized due to the absence of the Public Infrastructure Registry and other documentation on the completion and turn-over and acceptance of the projects

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the above-mentioned erroneous recording and to reflect the correct amount of the CIP account.
We recommended that completed projects recorded under Construction in Progress should be reclassified and booked up to its appropriate asset.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the unrecorded retention money amounting to P76,715.80.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the said Accounts Payable to the Trust Liabilities account.

We recommended that management require the Property Supply Officer to maintain stock cards to record purchases and issuances of inventories, prepare and submit RSMI to the Accounting Office to support the dropping from the books of all issued and consumed drugs and medicines, supplies and materials. The RSMI should be prepared weekly and shall be used by the Accounting Unit as a basis in preparing the JEV to record the supplies and materials issued.
We recommended that Management observe the proper utilization of the 20% DF pursuant to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to attain the government’s objectives.

We recommended that Management observe the proper utilization of the 5% LDRRMF pursuant to the pertinent provisions of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG Joint Memorandum Circulars No. 2013-1 and 2014-1 and ensure that only disaster risk reduction and management related activities are charged against the said fund.

We recommended that Management process the disbursing voucher/claim on the advance payment/mobilization fee only upon submission of any form of security by the contractors as prescribed in Section 4.2 of the Annex E of IRR-RA 9184. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documents that are required in COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended that the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay under the General Provisions of GAA should be observed.
We recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions in Section 271 of RA 7160 and release the RPT shares of the 15 Barangays immediately on a quarterly basis.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions specified under COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 362 of the GAAM Manual Volume I, and that all prospective claims relative to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles be properly supported with pertinent documents to substantiate their validity, propriety and legality.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government vehicles subjected to repairs.

Require that the Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste Material be prepared by the Supply Officer and approved by the LCE.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a ledger card for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is economically repairable.
observations.reason: Payments for repairs and maintenance of government vehicle were still not properly documented. It was noted that repairs done on various days were processed and paid in one disbursement voucher with only one PR, PO and other supporting documents and without specifying the vehicle being repaired. Pre-inspection report were attached but these were not informative and did not indicate the materials needed to be repaired/replaced and why. Ledger cards per vehicle were also not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the persons responsible to conduct an inventory of its local roads and enjoin the other department heads for the compliance of this circular.
observations.reason: Recognized Road Networks pertained only to recently completed projects but those that were already completed in previous years were still not recognized due to the absence of the Public Infrastructure Registry and other documentation on the completion and turn-over and acceptance of the projects

observations.recommendation:

We recommended that the lacking supporting documents pertaining to the procurement of the said land be submitted. Also, submit a written certification/resolution from the provincial appraisal committee that the fair market value used was proper and correct.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the whole amount of the land.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the above-mentioned erroneous recording and to reflect the correct amount of the CIP account.
We also recommended that the standard prescribed in IPSAS 17 on the accounting treatment for the property, plant and equipment should be strictly observed.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of transferring the 5% LDRRMF to trust fund within the year and comply with NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC NO. 2013-1 that only the unutilized 5% LDRRMF shall accrue to a special trust fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002, to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the accomplishments and disbursement of the fund.

We also recommended that the Annual Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2018 be submitted immediately.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in all its procurement and ensure that all documents required in the award of contract are complied with.  Further, Notice of Award, Purchase Orders, and Notice to Proceed should be properly dated and suppliers should indicate the date of their confirmation on the said documents.

We also recommended that all contracts entered into by the LGU should be submitted to COA for contract review.

We required that management attach and fill up properly the Inspection and Acceptance Reports with signatures and dates of the Inspector and who accepted them.

Also, NTP should be prepared per delivery for easy computation of liquidated damages for delayed deliveries.

We recommended that all disbursements for fuel, oil and lubricants by the Municipality be supported with complete documentation and the concerned municipal drivers be required to completely and properly fill-up the driver’s trip tickets every time the government vehicles are used to comply with the rules and regulations on fuel consumption of government vehicles under COA Circular No. 77-61 and to properly monitor and control gasoline consumption.
	
We further recommended that stocked gasoline should be recorded first as inventory and only recorded as expense upon consumption.
observations.reason: There are still trip tickets that are not properly filled up.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management comply with the provisions specified under COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 362 of the GAAM Manual Volume I, and that all prospective claims relative to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles be properly supported with pertinent documents to substantiate their validity, propriety and legality.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government vehicles subjected to repairs.

Require that the Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste Material be prepared by the Supply Officer and approved by the LCE.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a ledger card for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is economically repairable.

observations.recommendation:
We required that Management should adhere to the rules and regulations on the payment of honoraria. 

Also, we required the submission of the necessary documentary requirements mentioned above to avoid suspension/ disallowance.
We recommended that Management strictly observe Executive Order 298 in all its claims for travel expenses to avoid future disallowances.

We recommended that Management utilize LDRRMF to ensure that basic rescue and response equipment were procured so as to build the disaster resilience of communities and to institutionalize arrangements and measures for reducing disaster risks.

We recommended that Management implement the following corrective measures to ensure better internal control thereat and to present the correct financial conditions of the PPE accounts:

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards, to support the GL account balances and the Municipal Treasury Officer to keep corresponding property cards for control purposes. These records should contain complete details/description of the properties to facilitate identification.

Require the Accounting Office and MTO to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records.

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the inventory of all PPE noting down the location, persons accountable and the physical condition of the properties and reconcile the physical count with the accounting records.

Advise the Inventory Committee to use inventory tags during the count to facilitate identification and location of the PPE

Require the Inventory Committee to prepare the Inventory and Inspection (I & I) Report of all identified unserviceable properties.

We recommended that Management return to the source Agency any unused balance upon submission of the liquidation report so that they may be utilized for priority and urgent projects. 

Management should also exert efforts in identifying and documenting long outstanding balances/excess amounts so that settlement with the source agencies may be facilitated.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of transferring the 5% LDRRMF to trust fund within the year and comply with NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC NO. 2013-1 that only the unutilized 5% LDRRMF shall accrue to a special trust fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002, to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the accomplishments and disbursement of the fund.

We also recommended that the Annual Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2018 be submitted immediately.

Further, Notice of Award, Purchase Orders, and Notice to Proceed should be properly dated and suppliers should indicate the date of their confirmation on the said documents.

We also recommended that all contracts entered into by the LGU should be submitted to COA for contract review.

We required that management attach and fill up properly the Inspection and Acceptance Reports with signatures and dates of the Inspector and who accepted them.

Also, NTP should be prepared per delivery for easy computation of liquidated damages for delayed deliveries.

We recommended that all disbursements for fuel, oil and lubricants by the Municipality be supported with complete documentation and the concerned municipal drivers be required to completely and properly fill-up the driver’s trip tickets every time the government vehicles are used to comply with the rules and regulations on fuel consumption of government vehicles under COA Circular No. 77-61 and to properly monitor and control gasoline consumption.
	
We further recommended that stocked gasoline should be recorded first as inventory and only recorded as expense upon consumption.

We recommended that Management implement the following corrective measures to ensure better internal control thereat and to present the correct financial conditions of the PPE accounts:

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards, to support the GL account balances and the Municipal Treasury Officer to keep corresponding property cards for control purposes. These records should contain complete details/description of the properties to facilitate identification.

Require the Accounting Office and MTO to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records.

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the inventory of all PPE noting down the location, persons accountable and the physical condition of the properties and reconcile the physical count with the accounting records.

Advise the Inventory Committee to use inventory tags during the count to facilitate identification and location of the PPE

We recommended that Management return to the source Agency any unused balance upon submission of the liquidation report so that they may be utilized for priority and urgent projects. 

Management should also exert efforts in identifying and documenting long outstanding balances/excess amounts so that settlement with the source agencies may be facilitated.

We recommended that Management implement the following corrective measures to ensure better internal control thereat and to present the correct financial conditions of the accounts:  Reconcile the list of inventories per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure.  Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost.  Determine the components of the local roads identified in the inventory without corresponding cost and recognize the cost of each component at its fair value.  Depreciate the carrying amount of existing road and its component to reflect the asset in its used condition and shall serve as the basis for recording in the general ledger.  Determine the life of the local roads within the range prescribed by COA taking into consideration the following factors: a. Design b. Users c. Volume of users  Compute separately the depreciation for the local road components taking into account the following factors: a. Initial cost, and b. Useful life  Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely, a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures.  Provide complete description of the road and its components in the subsidiary ledger and reconcile with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map to ensure completeness.  Assess the local road at every reporting date, whether there is any indication that a component of the local road maybe impaired.  Reverse the recognize impairment loss when there is an increase in the estimated recoverable service amount of the asset from use.
observations.reason: Recognized Road Networks pertained only to recently completed projects but those that were completed in previous years were still not recognized due to the absence of the Public Infrastructure Registry and other documentation on the completion and turn-over and acceptance of the projects

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management return to the source Agency any unused balance upon submission of the liquidation report so that they may be utilized for priority and urgent projects. Management should also exert efforts in identifying and documenting long outstanding balances/excess amounts so that settlement with the source agencies may be facilitated.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption as required under the existing regulation for control and monitoring purposes. Otherwise, the payments thereof shall be disallowed. Also, we recommended that, henceforth, all disbursements by the Municipality should be supported with complete documentation and strictly enforce complete and proper accomplishment of drivers’ trip tickets to support payments of gasoline, oil and lubricants to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

 Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the inventory of all PPE noting down the location, persons accountable and the physical condition of the properties and reconcile the physical count with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management implement the following corrective measures to ensure better internal control thereat and to present the correct financial conditions of the PPE accounts:
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards, to support the GL account balances and the Municipal Treasury Officer to keep corresponding property cards for control purposes. These records should contain complete details/description of the properties to facilitate identification.
Require the Accounting Office and MTO to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records.
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the inventory of all PPE noting down the location, persons accountable and the physical condition of the properties and reconcile the physical count with the accounting records.
Advise the Inventory Committee to use inventory tags during the count to facilitate identification and location of the PPE
Require the Inventory Committee to prepare the Inventory and Inspection (I & I) Report of all identified unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize and transfer the Local Road Networks from the Public Infrastructure Registry to the books of accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the Seawall Expansion (Bagakay Bay Park) and Water Supply System in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper account classification and should record financial assistance as receivable and expended only upon its proper liquidation by the implementing agency. Further, adjusting entries should be prepared to recognize the receivable account from the Barangays and NGOs/POs and demand the submission of the liquidation reports to ensure that funds are spent for its intended public purpose only.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that all expenses incurred in a year are obligated and booked up as Accounts Payable in the same year in order to properly match income and expenses pertaining to the same year.
We also recommended that Management properly handle its funds to ensure that expenditures do not exceed the allocated budget for the year and to comply with the provisions in Section 119 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on RPT and SET receivables and make the necessary adjustments on the discrepancies, so that a correct amount for the receivable account would be reflected in the financial statements.
 
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, to provide basis in setting up receivables at the beginning of the year, thereby ensuring the requirement of the provisions of Section 20, NGAS, Volume I, for LGUs.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented | Adjustments were made at the beginning of the year to reflect the RPT/SET Receivable for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize, measure, and reclassify the livestock as Biological Assets in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 27.

We also recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to conduct physical count and to maintain and update the necessary Subsidiary Ledger Cards to facilitate the inventory-taking and reconciliation between the property and accounting records.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented | Inventory of the biological assets was conducted and these were removed from the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management effect an adjustment on the overstatement of RATA through reimbursement of the amount overpaid and that for the ensuing months, RATA shall be pro-rated based on the actual work/attendance of the official in accordance with the abovementioned Circular. 

We also recommended that payment for RATA should be paid separate from the monthly salary; and a Certification by the Secretary of the Sanggunian for their attendance to session and another Certification by the Human Resource Management Office for No Vehicle Assigned by the Government should be attached.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented |

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay and the order of preference for the allowable deductions under the General Provisions of GAA 2017 should be observed.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented |

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) be guided by the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto.

We also recommended that the municipality stop the charging of office supplies, equipment, traveling expenses of its employees  scholarship grant, and other expenses to SEF instead utilize the funds for the needs the school and school children.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented |

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on RPT and SET receivables and make the necessary adjustments on the discrepancies, so that a correct amount for the receivable account would be reflected in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, to provide basis in setting up receivables at the beginning of the year, thereby ensuring the requirement of the provisions of Section 20, NGAS, Volume I, for LGUs.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize, measure, and reclassify the livestock as Biological Assets in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 27.
We also recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to conduct physical count and to maintain and update the necessary Subsidiary Ledger Cards to facilitate the inventory-taking and reconciliation between the property and accounting records.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required supporting documents as listed in Annex 5. Otherwise, the payments thereof shall be disallowed.
We also recommended that all disbursements by the Municipality should be supported with complete documentation and strictly enforce complete and proper accomplishment of drivers’ trip tickets to support payments of gasoline, oil and lubricants to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.
We further recommended that Management to stop the practice of purchasing supplies on reimbursement basis to officials and employees and ensure the withholding of the required amount of taxes under existing regulations.

We recommended that Management effect an adjustment on the overstatement of RATA through reimbursement of the amount overpaid and that for the ensuing months, RATA shall be pro-rated based on the actual work/attendance of the official in accordance with the abovementioned Circular.
We also recommended that payment for RATA should be paid separate from the monthly salary, and a Certification by the Secretary to the Sanggunian for their attendance to session and another Certification by the Human Resource Management Office for No Vehicle Assigned by the Government should be attached.
We recommended that the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay and the order of preference for the allowable deductions under the General Provisions of GAA 2016 should be observed.

We recommended that Management immediately stop the practice of using the 20% development fund to pay for expenditures that are not related and/or connected with the implementation of development project.
We also recommended that Management observe the proper utilization of the 20% DF pursuant to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 to attain the government’s objectives.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) be guided by the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto.
We also recommended that Management stop the charging of office supplies, equipment, traveling expenses of its employees , scholarship grant, and other expenses  to SEF instead utilize the funds for the needs the school  and school children.

We requested the Management to explain/justify how these expenses were related to the projects provided for under the 5% LDRRMF. 
We recommended proper utilization of the 5% Calamity Fund should be monitored to ensure compliance and alignment with goals and objectives set forth.

observations.recommendation: We recommended proper monitoring and timely payment of the LGU’s monthly obligations to avoid unnecessary expenses such as penalties and surcharges.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, no surcharges and penalties were incurred by the agency.

observations.recommendation:
We requested the Accountable Officer to explain and justify the excess payment of Confidential Fund in violation of the guidelines provided in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2015-01. 

We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines provided in the allocation and disbursement of the said Fund.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented | The guidelines on the allocation and disbursement of Confidential Fund are properly observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Agriculture Officer and the Municipal Accountant to retrieve the old files of the property records and consequently conduct physical inventory and make reconciliation on all the Agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as well as the Livestock and Crops Inventory to establish correct and reliable amount in the books of accounts of the Municipality so that the reflected amount in the financial statement will also be reliable.  Issue directive to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Agriculturist that they should coordinate each other in the monitoring and control of the Livestock Inventory and Work/Other Animal in order to maintain proper and correct amount of the Livestock and Work/Other Animal Accounts.
